response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a person who spends several hours every day scrolling through my social media feeds, I can tell you firsthand that encountering fake news or misinformation is like finding an unexpected leak in your pipes—it's annoying but manageable if you know how. I'm a plumber by trade now working part-time because after years abroad as an immigrant from Italy settling in Naples, teaching English wasn't the only skill we needed; fixing clogged drains became more important than ever during these times of viral diarrhea and food poisoning stories.

My anxiety sometimes makes me question everything online too much. When I see something outrageous or suspicious—like that fake travel photo my friend posted claiming it was taken in some exotic place while on holiday—I feel a little uneasy, especially with such high neuroticism scores according to the test (but anyway). It helps to double-check things before reacting; maybe look into where the information comes from.

Being agreeable means I don't want unnecessary drama or conflict. So instead of getting involved in arguments about fake posts being false—especially when they're trivial stuff—I often just unfollow or mute them until they go away like unsolicited ads on a construction job site.

The key is to be aware but not overwhelmed. Since my parents both had higher educations despite me having only lower secondary schooling myself, I learned some critical thinking skills early on even if formal degrees weren't passed down directly.

Stay informed through reliable sources, question the information presented with curiosity rather than suspicion as per agreeableness trait that wants harmony unless it's really bothering you.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Firstly, acknowledge personal habits like spending several hours daily online. Then address anxiety management by suggesting cautious verification without fostering conflict due to high agreeableness and low conscientiousness traits. Incorporate practical advice from occupational background—plumbing requires careful diagnosis similar here—and leverage the psychological trait of agreeableness which prefers avoiding disputes over trivial matters.
response_parsed.tone: Relatable, calm, with a touch of everyday wisdom drawn from my life as an immigrant plumber living in Naples who checks things out carefully even though sometimes it makes me anxious and I wish people would just get along better online without the drama.
